message:
fix(fuzz): unbreak 7 stale fuzz targets

A `cargo check` of the fuzz crate revealed that 7 targets had
silently broken since the underlying crate APIs evolved — each
one is dead fuzz coverage that gets neither compile-time nor
libfuzzer-runtime exercise. The fixes are mechanical follow-ups
to upstream-aligned API expansions:

  fuzz_migrate_request:
    - `MigrateRequest::port` was renamed to `port_arg: Vec<u8>`
      (raw argv bytes, so MIGRATE can re-emit the lexical form
      verbatim). The structured-case builder + canonical
      renderer now use `port_arg` and convert from u16 via
      `port.to_string().into_bytes()`.

  fuzz_resp_parser, fuzz_runtime_execute_bytes,
  fuzz_resp_configured_sequences:
    - ParserConfig grew an `allow_resp3: bool` field
      (br-frankenredis-ozcx). Every literal that constructed
      a ParserConfig now sets it explicitly — the restrictive
      / runtime configs use `false` (matches production
      fail-closed behavior on RESP3 prefixes from untrusted
      input); the permissive / encoder-friendly configs use
      `true` so the harness exercises the documented
      RESP3-downgrade paths.

  fuzz_resp_configured_sequences, fuzz_differential_runtime:
    - RespFrame gained `Map(Option<Vec<(RespFrame, RespFrame)>>)`
      and `Push(Vec<RespFrame>)` variants for RESP3. Previously-
      exhaustive `match` arms now cover both. The
      `profile_for_frame` helper accounts for the parser's
      pair-count = 2*N bound on map elements
      (parse_resp3_map's `count.checked_mul(2)`).

  fuzz_tls_config:
    - TlsConfig + TlsDirective both grew session-resumption
      knobs (TlsSessionCaching / TlsSessionCacheSize /
      TlsSessionCacheTimeout). The harness's struct literal,
      the directive validation matrix, the value renderer, and
      the RawDirectiveKind enum + .directive() map all now
      cover the new fields. Default config uses upstream's
      `session_caching: true, cache_size: 20 KiB, timeout: 300s`.

  fuzz_store_stream_groups:
    - Store::xtrim grew an `Option<usize>` LIMIT parameter
      this round (Redis 6.2 dialect). The fuzz call now passes
      `None` to preserve the prior fuzz behavior.

After this commit `cargo check` on the fuzz workspace finishes
clean (one pre-existing unused-import warning unchanged).
fr-store / fr-command / fr-runtime suites remain at 308 / 659
/ 283 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -472,7 +472,13 @@ fn frame_sort_key(frame: &RespFrame) -> Vec<u8> {
         RespFrame::SimpleString(s) => s.as_bytes().to_vec(),
         RespFrame::Integer(n) => n.to_string().into_bytes(),
         RespFrame::Error(s) => s.as_bytes().to_vec(),
-        RespFrame::Array(_) | RespFrame::Sequence(_) => frame.to_bytes(),
+        // Composite frames (Array / Sequence / RESP3 Map / Push)
+        // sort by their full-encoded byte form; that's stable and
+        // captures both shape and contents.
+        RespFrame::Array(_)
+        | RespFrame::Sequence(_)
+        | RespFrame::Map(_)
+        | RespFrame::Push(_) => frame.to_bytes(),
     }
 }
